DEFENDANT FAILED TO SATISFY ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF MAKING A PRIMA FACIE CASE THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO PREVAIL ON ITS GOVERNMENT CONTRACTOR DEFENSE AS TO PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS BASED ON FAILURE TO WARN. HOWEVER THE MOTION AS A WHOLE MUST BE DENIED BECAUSE DEFENDANT DID NOT SEPARATELY SEEK SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFFS' CAUSES OF ACTION BASED SOLELY ON DESIGN DEFECT AND EXCLUDING FAILURE TO WARN. MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FRYER-KNOWLES, INC., A WASHINGTON CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: Dean Pollack of Burnham Brown (510.835.6705) for defendant and moving party; Roger Gold and Anya Fuchs of the Gold Law Firm (415.986.1338) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages are denied. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to plaintiffs' claims based on design defect and as to plaintiffs' claims based on failure to warn. As to summary adjudication, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant's failure to demonstrate that it propounded comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claim for punitive damages precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Thomas McCaffery are overruled. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tara L. Williams, CSR 13428 (Aiken Welch, 510.451.1580) (503/GLW)

APPEARANCES:
Meghan R. McMeel of SCHIFF HARDIN LLP (415-901-8763), appearing for defendant / moving party Owens-Illinois, Inc.

RULING:
DEFENDANT OWENS-ILLINOIS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, ADJUDICATION

No appearance of plaintiff as of 9:59 a.m. Due to Defendant's failure to notify plaintiffs' counsel and this Court of its intent to oppose this Court's tentative ruling on said motion pursuant to San Francisco Superior Court Uniform Local Rule 8.3, this Court accordingly adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' negligence and strict liability causes of action and claim for punitive damages are denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to plaintiffs' claims based on failure to warn. Defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to plaintiffs' claims based on design defect and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on that topic. However the motion as a whole must be denied because defendant did not separately seek summary adjudication of plaintiffs' causes of action based solely on design defect and excluding failure to warn. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Dawn Stark of US LEGAL SUPPORT, CSR #7847 (415-362-4346) (503/GLW)

Plaintiffs Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Specially Set A Preferential Trial Date Pursuant To C.C.P 36 (A) And (D)
Off Calendar Nov-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Roger E. Gold of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1338, X-134), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Osceola Heath & Bonnie Heath
Laura Przetak of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0425), Designated Defense Counsel
(Refer to Sign in Sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A PREFERENTIAL TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(a) and (d)

Hearing held. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as revised: Plaintiffs' motion to specially set a preferential trial date is granted pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(a) and (d). The trial date is February 29, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day of trial is March 18, 2016. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is Feb. 18, 2016. Plaintiffs and defendants have stipulated that motions for summary judgment/adjudication may be brought on 28 days notice, oppositions due 12 days before the hearing and replies due 5 days before the hearing. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Additionally, no later than 30 days before filing and serving a motion for summary judgment/adjudication, the moving party shall meet and confer with the other side advising of their intent to bring such a motion and the issue(s) that will be raised in the motion. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than January 8, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than January 22, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by Feb. 22, 2016 at 12:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on Feb. 24, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 5, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is Feb. 26, 2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 1, 2015. 2015. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than December 9, 2015 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than December 23, 2015 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on January 6, 2016 at 2:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry)
